prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of acrolein computed via DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: input structure provided as-is (single-point calculation, not re-optimized). SCF converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). Mulliken partial charges used to compute Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), and f⁰ (dual). The most electrophilic site is C3 (terminal vinyl carbon) with f⁺ = 0.642, indicating strong activation toward nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is O (carbonyl oxygen) with f⁻ = 0.394. These indices reflect acrolein's α,β-unsaturated carbonyl chemistry. Output plot and full results (Fukui indices for all 8 atoms, dual descriptors) are available in the calculation directory.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
